text: Patients on treatment_tamoxifen have different pfs_months compared to those not on treatment_tamoxifen.
rationale: Tamoxifen is an established endocrine therapy in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer, and treatment-associated PFS differences are standard clinical knowledge.

text: Patients on treatment_palbociclib have different pfs_months compared to those not on treatment_palbociclib.
rationale: Palbociclib plus endocrine therapy has well-established PFS benefit in HR-positive/HER2-negative advanced breast cancer.

text: Patients on treatment_trastuzumab have different pfs_months compared to those not on treatment_trastuzumab.
rationale: Trastuzumab benefit and PFS impact in HER2-positive breast cancer is a textbook and guideline-level association.

text: Patients on treatment_olaparib have different pfs_months compared to those not on treatment_olaparib.

text: Patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have different pfs_months compared to those not on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan has established PFS benefit in metastatic triple-negative and HR-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer settings.

text: Patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have different pfs_months compared to those not on treatment_pembrolizumab.
rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in selected breast cancer populations, especially PD-L1-positive triple-negative disease, is guideline-established.

text: Patients with sex_female have different pfs_months compared to those without sex_female.

text: Patients with stage_iv have different pfs_months compared to those without stage_iv.

text: Patients with has_brain_mets have different pfs_months compared to those without has_brain_mets.

text: Patients with node_positive have different pfs_months compared to those without node_positive.

text: Patients with postmenopausal have different pfs_months compared to those without postmenopausal.

text: Patients with er_positive have different pfs_months compared to those without er_positive.

text: Patients with pr_positive have different pfs_months compared to those without pr_positive.

text: Patients with her2_low have different pfs_months compared to those without her2_low.

text: Patients with brca1_mutation have different pfs_months compared to those without brca1_mutation.

text: Patients with brca2_mutation have different pfs_months compared to those without brca2_mutation.
rationale: BRCA2 mutation status is an established breast-cancer biomarker with recognized prognostic and therapeutic implications.

text: Patients with pik3ca_mutation have different pfs_months compared to those without pik3ca_mutation.

text: Patients with higher age_years have different pfs_months compared to those with lower age_years.

text: Patients with higher ecog_ps have different pfs_months compared to those with lower ecog_ps.

text: Patients with higher ki67_pct have different pfs_months compared to those with lower ki67_pct.

text: Patients with higher weight_loss_pct_6mo have different pfs_months compared to those with lower weight_loss_pct_6mo.

text: Patients with higher crp_mg_l have different pfs_months compared to those with lower crp_mg_l.
rationale: CRP is an established systemic inflammation marker commonly linked to poorer cancer outcomes.

text: Patients with higher nlr have different pfs_months compared to those with lower nlr.

text: Patients with higher hemoglobin_g_dl have different pfs_months compared to those with lower hemoglobin_g_dl.

text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by her2_positive status.

text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by brca1_mutation status.

text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by brca2_mutation status.
rationale: BRCA2 mutation modifying benefit from olaparib is an established PARP-inhibitor biomarker paradigm in oncology.

text: The effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months differs by er_positive status.
rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-positive breast cancer is a standard CDK4/6 inhibitor treatment association.

text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by stage_iv status.
rationale: Stage IV status as a modifier of pembrolizumab effect on PFS is not a canonical predictive biomarker like PD-L1, MSI-H, or TMB.

text: Refined: The Mean pfs_months: 5.41 vs 4.31 (t=47.95, p=0.00) is statistically significant.
rationale: A bare statistically significant PFS mean difference without a specified biological or treatment context is not a novel oncology hypothesis.

text: Refined: The Mean pfs_months: 4.64 vs 4.71 (t=-2.17, p=0.03) is statistically significant.
rationale: A small generic statistically significant PFS mean difference without named covariates or mechanism does not advance a novel oncology claim.

text: Refined: The Mean pfs_months: 4.63 vs 4.70 (t=-2.38, p=0.02) is statistically significant.
rationale: This is only an unspecified statistical PFS comparison and lacks a disease, biomarker, treatment, or mechanism that could be judged novel.

text: Refined: The Mean pfs_months: 3.62 vs 5.15 (t=-65.70, p=0.00) is statistically significant.
rationale: A statistically significant PFS mean contrast alone is descriptive and does not state a novel oncology or functional-genomics association.

text: Refined: The Mean pfs_months: 3.75 vs 4.80 (t=-28.68, p=0.00) is statistically significant.
rationale: The hypothesis gives only an unspecified PFS mean difference, so it is not a substantive novel oncology claim.

text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months varies by postmenopausal.
rationale: Menopausal status influencing endocrine therapy choice or tamoxifen context is a standard breast oncology paradigm.

text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months varies by er_positive.
rationale: ER positivity predicting tamoxifen benefit is a foundational and well-established breast cancer treatment association.

text: The effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months varies by ki67_pct.
rationale: Ki-67 is an established proliferation/prognostic marker, but a specific Ki-67-dependent variation in palbociclib PFS benefit is not a settled guideline-level predictive paradigm.

text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months varies by tumor_size_cm.
rationale: Tumor size is a standard prognostic and staging factor, but it is not an established modifier of trastuzumab PFS benefit.

text: Higher age_years is associated with different pfs_months.
rationale: Age is a common clinical prognostic covariate in oncology outcomes analyses and does not by itself constitute a novel cancer biology or treatment-response claim.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with different pfs_months.
rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-established prognostic factor for progression and survival outcomes across oncology.

text: Higher ki67_pct is associated with different pfs_months.
rationale: Ki-67 is a standard proliferation marker with established prognostic relevance in breast cancer and related oncology contexts.

text: Higher tumor_size_cm is associated with different pfs_months.
rationale: Tumor size is a textbook staging and prognostic factor, so association with PFS is not novel.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with different pfs_months.
rationale: Serum albumin is a well-known general prognostic marker of nutritional and inflammatory status in cancer outcomes.

text: Refined: The interaction effect is significant: Interaction effect (modifier=her2_positive=1): -0.08 vs -0.05, diff=-0.03
rationale: HER2 positivity as a treatment-relevant modifier, especially for HER2-targeted therapy contexts, is an established breast oncology paradigm.

text: Refined: The interaction effect is significant: Interaction effect (modifier=er_positive=1): 1.58 vs -0.02, diff=1.59
rationale: ER positivity as a determinant of endocrine/CDK4/6 treatment context and prognosis is a standard breast cancer paradigm.

text: Refined: The interaction effect is significant: Interaction effect (modifier=stage_iv=1): -0.03 vs -0.09, diff=0.06
rationale: A specific stage IV-dependent interaction effect of this magnitude is a subgroup-conditional treatment-effect claim rather than a settled guideline-level association.

text: Overall, 26/50 analyses showed statistically significant associations with pfs_months.
rationale: This is only a descriptive summary of statistical results across analyses and does not state a substantive oncology or functional-genomics association beyond established knowledge.

text: Patients with treatment_enzalutamide set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with treatment_enzalutamide set to 0.
rationale: Enzalutamide is an established prostate cancer therapy with well-known response associations, so a treatment-versus-no-treatment objective response comparison is not novel.

text: Patients with treatment_abiraterone set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with treatment_abiraterone set to 0.
rationale: Abiraterone is a standard androgen-signaling therapy in prostate cancer, making differential response by treatment status an established clinical question rather than a novel hypothesis.

text: Patients with treatment_docetaxel set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with treatment_docetaxel set to 0.
rationale: Docetaxel is a standard prostate cancer systemic therapy with known response expectations, so comparing objective response by receipt of docetaxel is not novel.

text: Patients with treatment_olaparib set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with treatment_olaparib set to 0.
rationale: Olaparib response in biomarker-selected prostate cancer, especially homologous recombination repair altered disease, is guideline-established and this broad treatment-response contrast is not novel.

text: Patients with treatment_lu177_psma set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with treatment_lu177_psma set to 0.
rationale: Lu-177 PSMA radioligand therapy is an established prostate cancer treatment with recognized response activity, so treatment status versus objective response is not novel.

text: Patients with treatment_pembrolizumab set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with treatment_pembrolizumab set to 0.
rationale: Pembrolizumab has established prostate cancer indications in MSI-high, TMB-high, or mismatch-repair deficient tumors, making a generic treatment-response association non-novel.

text: Patients with age_years set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with age_years set to 0.
rationale: Age is a routine clinical covariate in oncology outcomes and treatment-response analyses, so a simple age-stratified response difference is not novel.

text: Patients with sex_female set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with sex_female set to 0.
rationale: Sex is a standard demographic covariate, and in prostate cancer female sex coding is not a new mechanistic or subgroup-specific oncology insight.

text: Patients with ecog_ps set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with ecog_ps set to 0.
rationale: ECOG performance status is a standard prognostic and treatment-stratification variable in oncology, so association with objective response is not novel.

text: Patients with gleason_score set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with gleason_score set to 0.
rationale: Gleason score is a canonical prostate cancer risk and prognostic feature, making a simple response association established rather than novel.

text: Patients with psa_ng_ml set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with psa_ng_ml set to 0.
rationale: PSA level is a standard prostate-cancer disease-burden and monitoring variable, so a response-rate association would not be novel without a more specific mechanism or context.

text: Patients with brca2_mutation set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with brca2_mutation set to 0.
rationale: BRCA2 mutation is an established predictive biomarker in prostate cancer, especially for PARP inhibitor and DNA-damaging therapy sensitivity.

text: Patients with ar_v7_positive set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with ar_v7_positive set to 0.
rationale: AR-V7 positivity is a well-established marker of altered response to androgen-receptor pathway inhibitors in advanced prostate cancer.

text: Patients with msi_high set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with msi_high set to 0.
rationale: MSI-high status is an established pan-cancer and prostate-cancer immunotherapy response biomarker.

text: Patients with psma_high set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with psma_high set to 0.
rationale: High PSMA expression is an established selection and response-related biomarker for PSMA-targeted imaging and radioligand therapy in prostate cancer.

text: Patients with mcrpc set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with mcrpc set to 0.
rationale: mCRPC status is a standard prostate-cancer disease state used to stratify expected treatment response and prognosis.

text: Patients with visceral_mets set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with visceral_mets set to 0.
rationale: Visceral metastases are a well-known adverse clinical feature associated with prognosis and treatment outcomes in advanced prostate cancer.

text: Patients with albumin_g_dl set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with albumin_g_dl set to 0.
rationale: A binary albumin-defined subgroup difference in objective response is a nonspecific clinical-laboratory association rather than a standard prostate-cancer predictive paradigm.

text: Patients with ldh_u_l set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with ldh_u_l set to 0.
rationale: LDH is a widely recognized adverse prognostic marker in advanced oncology and metastatic prostate cancer outcome analyses.

text: Patients with crp_mg_l set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with crp_mg_l set to 0.
rationale: A CRP-defined subgroup difference in objective response is an inflammatory-marker association that is not a standard guideline-level predictive biomarker for prostate cancer.

text: Patients with nlr set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with nlr set to 0.
rationale: NLR is a widely studied inflammatory prognostic and response-associated marker in advanced prostate cancer, so a simple response-rate difference by NLR status is not novel.

text: Patients with hemoglobin_g_dl set to 1 have different objective_response rates than those with hemoglobin_g_dl set to 0.
rationale: Hemoglobin or anemia status is an established clinical prognostic factor in advanced prostate cancer, so a simple association with objective response is not novel.

text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies by age_years.
rationale: Age-dependent modification of enzalutamide objective response is a subgroup-interaction claim rather than a settled guideline or textbook association.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by age_years.
rationale: Age-dependent modification of abiraterone objective response is not a standard consensus biomarker or treatment-selection rule.

text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response varies by age_years.

text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response varies by age_years.

text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response varies by age_years.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by visceral_mets.

text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response varies by gleason_score.

text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response varies by visceral_mets.

text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies by brca2_mutation.

text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies by ar_v7_positive.
rationale: AR-V7 positivity is a well-established marker associated with resistance or reduced response to androgen-receptor pathway inhibitors such as enzalutamide.

text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies by msi_high.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by brca2_mutation.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by ar_v7_positive.
rationale: AR-V7 positivity is a well-established marker associated with resistance or reduced response to androgen-receptor pathway inhibitors including abiraterone.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by msi_high.
rationale: MSI-high status is a recognized checkpoint-inhibitor biomarker, not an established modifier of abiraterone objective response.

text: Patients with treatment_enzalutamide=1 AND age_years<65 have different objective_response rates than other patients with treatment_enzalutamide=1.
rationale: An age-under-65 subgroup difference in objective response among enzalutamide-treated patients is not a standard prostate cancer treatment-response paradigm.

text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies by albumin_g_dl.

text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies by ldh_u_l.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by albumin_g_dl.
rationale: Abiraterone response modification by serum albumin is a clinical biomarker interaction claim not established as a standard predictive oncology or functional-genomics consensus.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by ldh_u_l.
rationale: LDH is a known prognostic marker in advanced cancer, but LDH-specific variation in objective response to abiraterone is not a standard established predictive association.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response varies by crp_mg_l.
rationale: CRP reflects systemic inflammation and prognosis, but CRP-dependent objective-response benefit from abiraterone is not established guideline or textbook consensus.

text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is strongest in patients with age_years<65 AND gleason_score>7.
rationale: A subgroup claim that enzalutamide response is strongest in younger patients with high Gleason score is not a standard established predictive rule.

text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is strongest in patients with age_years<65 AND gleason_score>7.
rationale: A subgroup claim that abiraterone response is strongest in younger patients with high Gleason score goes beyond standard prostate cancer treatment-response consensus.

text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is strongest in patients with age_years<65 AND gleason_score>7.
rationale: A subgroup claim that docetaxel objective response is strongest in younger patients with high Gleason score is not an established canonical predictive association.

text: Treatment effects on objective_response vary systematically by patient characteristics.
rationale: The broad statement that treatment effects can vary by patient characteristics is a generic clinical principle rather than a novel oncology hypothesis.
